metadata.title: Prefect Flow Connectivity — MLflow as Inter-Flow Contract
metadata.priority: P0 — pipeline produces no real results without this fix

setup.step: Create GitHub issue for Phase 0 (broken seam fix)
Create GitHub issue for Phase 1 (data-flow → train-flow handoff)
Load state/tdd-state.json — reset to this plan

phase.name: Fix Core Seam
phase.description:
Minimum viable fix so post-training plugins actually execute and analysis evaluates real models. The debug run should show: - post-training: plugins execute (not skipped) - analysis: ≥1 model evaluated, champion tagged in MLflow
Root cause summary: 1. train_flow.py never logs checkpoint_dir as an MLflow tag on fold runs 2. FlowContract.log_flow_completion() uses tag "completed" not "FLOW_COMPLETE" 3. FlowContract has no find_fold_checkpoints() method 4. constants.py has no resolve_experiment_name() utility 5. analysis_flow.py hardcodes "minivess_training" (breaks with _DEBUG suffix) 6. post_training_flow.py has checkpoint_paths=[] default (all plugins skip)

task.name: Tests for FlowContract + resolve_experiment_name
task.description:
Write failing unit tests for: 1. resolve_experiment_name() in constants.py 2. FlowContract._debug_suffix from MINIVESS_DEBUG_SUFFIX env var 3. FlowContract.find_fold_checkpoints() new method 4. FlowContract.log_flow_completion() writing FLOW_COMPLETE (not "completed") and accepting checkpoint_dir kwarg
These tests must FAIL before T0.2 (GREEN) implements the code.
task.test-first:
Create tests/unit/orchestration/test_flow_contract_connectivity.py
Use pytest fixtures. All tests use MINIVESS_ALLOW_HOST=1 env and a tmp_path-based mlflow tracking URI (mlflow.set_tracking_uri(str(tmp_path))). Do NOT mock mlflow — use a real local mlflow file store.
Required test functions:
class TestResolveExperimentName: def test_no_suffix_when_env_unset(monkeypatch): monkeypatch.delenv("MINIVESS_DEBUG_SUFFIX", raising=False) from minivess.orchestration.constants import resolve_experiment_name assert resolve_experiment_name("minivess_training") == "minivess_training"
def test_debug_suffix_appended_when_env_set(monkeypatch): monkeypatch.setenv("MINIVESS_DEBUG_SUFFIX", "_DEBUG") from minivess.orchestration.constants import resolve_experiment_name assert resolve_experiment_name("minivess_training") == "minivess_training_DEBUG"
def test_custom_suffix(monkeypatch): monkeypatch.setenv("MINIVESS_DEBUG_SUFFIX", "_CI") from minivess.orchestration.constants import resolve_experiment_name assert resolve_experiment_name("minivess_data") == "minivess_data_CI"
class TestFlowContractDebugSuffix: def test_debug_suffix_empty_when_env_unset(monkeypatch, tmp_path): monkeypatch.delenv("MINIVESS_DEBUG_SUFFIX", raising=False) from minivess.orchestration.flow_contract import FlowContract fc = FlowContract(tracking_uri=str(tmp_path / "mlruns")) assert fc._debug_suffix == ""
def test_debug_suffix_set_from_env(monkeypatch, tmp_path): monkeypatch.setenv("MINIVESS_DEBUG_SUFFIX", "_DEBUG") from minivess.orchestration.flow_contract import FlowContract fc = FlowContract(tracking_uri=str(tmp_path / "mlruns")) assert fc._debug_suffix == "_DEBUG"
def test_resolve_experiment_uses_suffix(monkeypatch, tmp_path): monkeypatch.setenv("MINIVESS_DEBUG_SUFFIX", "_DEBUG") from minivess.orchestration.flow_contract import FlowContract fc = FlowContract(tracking_uri=str(tmp_path / "mlruns")) assert fc._resolve_experiment("minivess_training") == "minivess_training_DEBUG"
class TestLogFlowCompletionWritesFLOW_COMPLETE: def test_log_completion_writes_FLOW_COMPLETE_tag(tmp_path): """log_flow_completion must set flow_status=FLOW_COMPLETE (not 'completed').""" import mlflow from mlflow.tracking import MlflowClient from minivess.orchestration.flow_contract import FlowContract
tracking_uri = str(tmp_path / "mlruns") mlflow.set_tracking_uri(tracking_uri) mlflow.set_experiment("test_exp") with mlflow.start_run() as run: run_id = run.info.run_id
fc = FlowContract(tracking_uri=tracking_uri) fc.log_flow_completion(flow_name="test-flow", run_id=run_id)
client = MlflowClient(tracking_uri=tracking_uri) run_data = client.get_run(run_id) assert run_data.data.tags["flow_status"] == "FLOW_COMPLETE"
def test_log_completion_with_checkpoint_dir(tmp_path): """log_flow_completion must tag checkpoint_dir when provided.""" from pathlib import Path import mlflow from mlflow.tracking import MlflowClient from minivess.orchestration.flow_contract import FlowContract
tracking_uri = str(tmp_path / "mlruns") mlflow.set_tracking_uri(tracking_uri) mlflow.set_experiment("test_exp") with mlflow.start_run() as run: run_id = run.info.run_id
ckpt_dir = tmp_path / "checkpoints" / "fold_0" fc = FlowContract(tracking_uri=tracking_uri) fc.log_flow_completion( flow_name="training-flow", run_id=run_id, checkpoint_dir=ckpt_dir, )
client = MlflowClient(tracking_uri=tracking_uri) run_data = client.get_run(run_id) assert run_data.data.tags["checkpoint_dir"] == str(ckpt_dir) assert run_data.data.tags["flow_status"] == "FLOW_COMPLETE"
class TestFindFoldCheckpoints: def test_find_fold_checkpoints_returns_empty_when_no_runs(tmp_path): from minivess.orchestration.flow_contract import FlowContract fc = FlowContract(tracking_uri=str(tmp_path / "mlruns")) result = fc.find_fold_checkpoints(parent_run_id="nonexistent_id") assert result == []
def test_find_fold_checkpoints_returns_checkpoint_dirs(tmp_path): """When fold runs have parent_run_id tag + checkpoint_dir tag, find them.""" import mlflow from mlflow.tracking import MlflowClient from minivess.orchestration.flow_contract import FlowContract
tracking_uri = str(tmp_path / "mlruns") mlflow.set_tracking_uri(tracking_uri) mlflow.set_experiment("minivess_training")
# Create a fake parent run with mlflow.start_run(tags={"flow_name": "training-flow"}) as parent: parent_run_id = parent.info.run_id
# Create two fake fold runs tagged with parent_run_id ckpt_dirs = [] fold_run_ids = [] for fold_id in range(2): ckpt_dir = tmp_path / f"checkpoints/fold_{fold_id}" ckpt_dir.mkdir(parents=True) # Write a fake checkpoint file (ckpt_dir / "best.ckpt").write_text("fake", encoding="utf-8") with mlflow.start_run( tags={ "parent_run_id": parent_run_id, "fold_id": str(fold_id), "checkpoint_dir": str(ckpt_dir), "flow_name": "training-flow", "flow_status": "FLOW_COMPLETE", } ) as fold_run: fold_run_ids.append(fold_run.info.run_id) ckpt_dirs.append(ckpt_dir)
# Mark fold runs as FINISHED client = MlflowClient(tracking_uri=tracking_uri) for fold_run_id in fold_run_ids: client.set_terminated(fold_run_id, status="FINISHED")
fc = FlowContract(tracking_uri=tracking_uri) results = fc.find_fold_checkpoints(parent_run_id=parent_run_id)
assert len(results) == 2 returned_dirs = {Path(r["checkpoint_dir"]) for r in results} assert returned_dirs == set(ckpt_dirs) assert all("fold_id" in r for r in results) assert all("run_id" in r for r in results)
task.acceptance: uv run pytest tests/unit/orchestration/test_flow_contract_connectivity.py -v All 10 tests FAIL (ImportError or AssertionError) — FlowContract lacks the new methods.

task.name: Implement FlowContract enhancements + resolve_experiment_name
task.description: Implement minimum code to make T0.1 tests pass. Do NOT add unused features. Every line of code must be tested by T0.1.
task.implementation:
1. src/minivess/orchestration/constants.py Add after the FLOW_NAME_* constants:
import os
def resolve_experiment_name(base_name: str) -> str: """Append MINIVESS_DEBUG_SUFFIX to experiment name if set.""" suffix = os.environ.get("MINIVESS_DEBUG_SUFFIX", "") return f"{base_name}{suffix}"
2. src/minivess/orchestration/flow_contract.py a) Add `import os` at top b) Update __init__: self._debug_suffix = os.environ.get("MINIVESS_DEBUG_SUFFIX", "") c) Add _resolve_experiment() method: def _resolve_experiment(self, base_name: str) -> str: return f"{base_name}{self._debug_suffix}" d) Update log_flow_completion() signature: def log_flow_completion( self, *, flow_name: str, run_id: str, checkpoint_dir: Path | None = None, artifacts: list[str] | None = None, ) -> None: - Change: client.set_tag(run_id, "flow_status", "FLOW_COMPLETE") (was "completed" — fix the typo) - Add: if checkpoint_dir is not None: client.set_tag(run_id, "checkpoint_dir", str(checkpoint_dir)) e) Add find_fold_checkpoints() method: def find_fold_checkpoints( self, *, parent_run_id: str, ) -> list[dict[str, Any]]: """Find all fold runs tagged with parent_run_id. Returns [{fold_id, run_id, checkpoint_dir}, ...] Queries all experiments for runs with parent_run_id tag. Returns empty list on any failure. """ try: from mlflow.tracking import MlflowClient client = MlflowClient(tracking_uri=self.tracking_uri) # Search across all experiments for fold runs of this parent runs = client.search_runs( experiment_ids=[], # search all experiments filter_string=f"tags.parent_run_id = '{parent_run_id}' " f"AND attributes.status = 'FINISHED'", order_by=["tags.fold_id ASC"], ) result = [] for run in runs: tags = run.data.tags if "checkpoint_dir" not in tags: continue result.append({ "run_id": run.info.run_id, "fold_id": int(tags.get("fold_id", "0")), "checkpoint_dir": Path(tags["checkpoint_dir"]), }) return result except Exception: logger.warning("find_fold_checkpoints failed", exc_info=True) return []
NOTE: search_runs with experiment_ids=[] may not be supported in all MLflow versions. If it fails, use a known experiment name. Check MLflow docs/source. Alternative: accept experiment_name as a parameter and resolve it internally using _resolve_experiment("minivess_training").
IMPORTANT: Add `from pathlib import Path` import to flow_contract.py if not present.
task.acceptance: uv run pytest tests/unit/orchestration/test_flow_contract_connectivity.py -v All 10 tests PASS. uv run ruff check src/minivess/orchestration/flow_contract.py src/minivess/orchestration/constants.py uv run mypy src/minivess/orchestration/flow_contract.py src/minivess/orchestration/constants.py

task.name: train_flow logs checkpoint_dir per fold + FLOW_COMPLETE
task.description:
RED: Write tests verifying train_flow logs required tags. GREEN: Add the 3 missing tag writes to train_flow.py.
Current gaps in train_flow.py (verified from code): - checkpoint_dir computed as `checkpoint_base / f"fold_{fold_id}"` at line 604 but NEVER logged to MLflow - log_fold_results_task() logs metrics only (val_loss, final_epoch) - parent MLflow run has no flow_status=FLOW_COMPLETE tag - fold runs have no parent_run_id tag (makes find_fold_checkpoints impossible)
IMPORTANT: The current code opens ONE parent MLflow run for ALL folds, then logs fold metrics into it. We need to change the architecture so that: - Each fold gets its OWN MLflow run (child run) with checkpoint_dir tag - Parent run gets flow_status=FLOW_COMPLETE at end
However: changing to per-fold runs is a bigger refactor. MINIMUM FIX for Phase 0: Log fold-specific tags on the PARENT run using fold-indexed tag names: checkpoint_dir_fold_0, checkpoint_dir_fold_1, etc. fold_run_status_0, fold_run_status_1, etc. This is simpler and doesn't change the run architecture. find_fold_checkpoints() adapts to read these parent-run tags instead.
REVISION to T0.1: If per-fold runs are kept as parent-run tags, update find_fold_checkpoints() to query parent run tags instead of searching for child runs. This is the minimal correct approach.
task.test-first:
Create tests/unit/orchestration/test_train_flow_tags.py
Since train_flow calls actual GPU training, write tests that exercise the MLflow tagging logic WITHOUT running training: - Mock or stub train_one_fold_task to return a fake result dict - Use MINIVESS_ALLOW_HOST=1 and a tmp_path mlflow tracking URI
class TestTrainFlowMLflowTags: @pytest.fixture def mock_fold_result(): return { "best_val_loss": 0.5, "final_epoch": 10, "history": {"val_loss": [0.8, 0.6, 0.5]}, }
def test_log_fold_results_task_logs_checkpoint_dir(tmp_path, mock_fold_result): """log_fold_results_task must set checkpoint_dir_{fold_id} tag on run.""" import mlflow from mlflow.tracking import MlflowClient from minivess.orchestration.flows.train_flow import log_fold_results_task
tracking_uri = str(tmp_path / "mlruns") mlflow.set_tracking_uri(tracking_uri) mlflow.set_experiment("test_train") ckpt_dir = tmp_path / "checkpoints" / "fold_0" ckpt_dir.mkdir(parents=True)
with mlflow.start_run() as run: run_id = run.info.run_id log_fold_results_task( fold_id=0, result=mock_fold_result, mlflow_run_id=run_id, checkpoint_dir=ckpt_dir, # NEW parameter )
client = MlflowClient(tracking_uri=tracking_uri) run_data = client.get_run(run_id) assert run_data.data.tags.get("checkpoint_dir_fold_0") == str(ckpt_dir)
def test_log_fold_results_task_logs_fold_id_tag(tmp_path, mock_fold_result): """log_fold_results_task must tag fold_id_0, fold_id_1 etc.""" import mlflow from mlflow.tracking import MlflowClient from minivess.orchestration.flows.train_flow import log_fold_results_task
tracking_uri = str(tmp_path / "mlruns") mlflow.set_tracking_uri(tracking_uri) mlflow.set_experiment("test_train") ckpt_dir = tmp_path / "checkpoints" / "fold_1" ckpt_dir.mkdir(parents=True)
with mlflow.start_run() as run: run_id = run.info.run_id log_fold_results_task( fold_id=1, result=mock_fold_result, mlflow_run_id=run_id, checkpoint_dir=ckpt_dir, )
client = MlflowClient(tracking_uri=tracking_uri) run_data = client.get_run(run_id) assert "checkpoint_dir_fold_1" in run_data.data.tags
def test_training_flow_result_has_checkpoint_dirs(tmp_path): """TrainingFlowResult must expose checkpoint_dirs dict.""" from minivess.orchestration.flows.train_flow import TrainingFlowResult result = TrainingFlowResult( flow_name="train", n_folds=2, loss_name="dice_ce", model_family="dynunet", checkpoint_dirs={0: tmp_path / "fold_0", 1: tmp_path / "fold_1"}, ) assert result.checkpoint_dirs[0] == tmp_path / "fold_0" assert len(result.checkpoint_dirs) == 2
Also add to TestFindFoldCheckpoints in test_flow_contract_connectivity.py: def test_find_fold_checkpoints_from_parent_run_tags(tmp_path): """find_fold_checkpoints must read checkpoint_dir_fold_N tags from parent run.""" import mlflow from mlflow.tracking import MlflowClient from minivess.orchestration.flow_contract import FlowContract
tracking_uri = str(tmp_path / "mlruns") mlflow.set_tracking_uri(tracking_uri) mlflow.set_experiment("minivess_training")
ckpt_dir_0 = tmp_path / "checkpoints/fold_0" ckpt_dir_1 = tmp_path / "checkpoints/fold_1" ckpt_dir_0.mkdir(parents=True) ckpt_dir_1.mkdir(parents=True)
with mlflow.start_run( tags={ "flow_name": "training-flow", "checkpoint_dir_fold_0": str(ckpt_dir_0), "checkpoint_dir_fold_1": str(ckpt_dir_1), "n_folds": "2", } ) as run: parent_run_id = run.info.run_id
client = MlflowClient(tracking_uri=tracking_uri) client.set_terminated(parent_run_id, status="FINISHED")
fc = FlowContract(tracking_uri=tracking_uri) results = fc.find_fold_checkpoints(parent_run_id=parent_run_id)
assert len(results) == 2 dirs = {r["checkpoint_dir"] for r in results} assert ckpt_dir_0 in dirs assert ckpt_dir_1 in dirs
task.implementation:
1. src/minivess/orchestration/flows/train_flow.py a) Update log_fold_results_task signature to accept checkpoint_dir: def log_fold_results_task( fold_id: int, result: dict[str, Any], mlflow_run_id: str | None, checkpoint_dir: Path | None = None, # NEW ) -> None:
b) Inside the try block, after existing metric logging, add: if checkpoint_dir is not None: client.set_tag(run_id, f"checkpoint_dir_fold_{fold_id}", str(checkpoint_dir))
c) Update the caller in training_flow(): for fold_id, fold_result in enumerate(fold_results): log_fold_results_task( fold_id, fold_result, mlflow_run_id, checkpoint_dir=checkpoint_base / f"fold_{fold_id}", # ADD )
d) After the for loop logging folds, add: mlflow.set_tag("n_folds", str(len(fold_results)))
e) After mlflow run context closes, flow_status tag is written by log_completion_safe (already present). Verify it uses "FLOW_COMPLETE" after T0.2 fixes the value.
2. src/minivess/orchestration/flows/train_flow.py Update TrainingFlowResult dataclass to include checkpoint_dirs field: @dataclass class TrainingFlowResult: ... checkpoint_dirs: dict[int, Path] = field(default_factory=dict)
3. src/minivess/orchestration/flow_contract.py Update find_fold_checkpoints() to also read parent-run tags (checkpoint_dir_fold_0, checkpoint_dir_fold_1, etc.) as an alternative to child-run tags. Check both approaches: if tags on parent run, use those; if fold runs with parent_run_id tag exist, use those. This makes the method backward-compatible with both designs.
task.acceptance: uv run pytest tests/unit/orchestration/test_train_flow_tags.py -v uv run pytest tests/unit/orchestration/test_flow_contract_connectivity.py -v All tests pass. uv run ruff check src/minivess/orchestration/flows/train_flow.py uv run mypy src/minivess/orchestration/flows/train_flow.py

task.name: post_training_flow discovers checkpoints via FlowContract
task.description:
RED: Write tests verifying post_training_flow resolves checkpoint_paths from FlowContract.find_fold_checkpoints() when none are explicitly provided.
GREEN: Update post_training_flow.py so that when checkpoint_paths is None or empty, it calls find_fold_checkpoints(parent_run_id=upstream_training_run_id) and resolves .ckpt files on the volume.
task.test-first:
Create tests/unit/orchestration/test_post_training_contract.py
def test_post_training_resolves_checkpoints_from_flow_contract( tmp_path, monkeypatch ): """When checkpoint_paths=None, post_training must query FlowContract.""" import mlflow from mlflow.tracking import MlflowClient from unittest.mock import patch, MagicMock from minivess.orchestration.flows.post_training_flow import ( resolve_checkpoint_paths_from_contract, # new helper function )
tracking_uri = str(tmp_path / "mlruns") monkeypatch.setenv("MINIVESS_ALLOW_HOST", "1")
# Create fake fold checkpoint files ckpt_dir_0 = tmp_path / "checkpoints" / "fold_0" ckpt_dir_0.mkdir(parents=True) ckpt_file = ckpt_dir_0 / "best.ckpt" ckpt_file.write_text("fake", encoding="utf-8")
# Mock FlowContract.find_fold_checkpoints to return our fake checkpoint with patch( "minivess.orchestration.flow_contract.FlowContract.find_fold_checkpoints" ) as mock_find: mock_find.return_value = [ {"fold_id": 0, "run_id": "fake_run", "checkpoint_dir": ckpt_dir_0} ] paths = resolve_checkpoint_paths_from_contract( parent_run_id="fake_parent", tracking_uri=tracking_uri, )
assert len(paths) == 1 assert paths[0] == ckpt_file
def test_resolve_checkpoint_paths_returns_empty_when_no_upstream( tmp_path, monkeypatch ): """When no upstream run found, returns empty list (graceful).""" from unittest.mock import patch from minivess.orchestration.flows.post_training_flow import ( resolve_checkpoint_paths_from_contract, )
monkeypatch.setenv("MINIVESS_ALLOW_HOST", "1")
with patch( "minivess.orchestration.flow_contract.FlowContract.find_fold_checkpoints" ) as mock_find: mock_find.return_value = [] paths = resolve_checkpoint_paths_from_contract( parent_run_id=None, tracking_uri=str(tmp_path / "mlruns"), )
assert paths == []
def test_resolve_checkpoint_globs_best_ckpt(tmp_path, monkeypatch): """resolve_checkpoint_paths_from_contract globs *.ckpt from checkpoint_dir.""" from unittest.mock import patch from minivess.orchestration.flows.post_training_flow import ( resolve_checkpoint_paths_from_contract, )
monkeypatch.setenv("MINIVESS_ALLOW_HOST", "1")
ckpt_dir = tmp_path / "fold_0" ckpt_dir.mkdir(parents=True) (ckpt_dir / "epoch_005.ckpt").write_text("a", encoding="utf-8") (ckpt_dir / "best.ckpt").write_text("b", encoding="utf-8")
with patch( "minivess.orchestration.flow_contract.FlowContract.find_fold_checkpoints" ) as mock_find: mock_find.return_value = [ {"fold_id": 0, "run_id": "r0", "checkpoint_dir": ckpt_dir} ] paths = resolve_checkpoint_paths_from_contract( parent_run_id="fake", tracking_uri=str(tmp_path / "mlruns"), )
# Should find best.ckpt (prefer best.ckpt over epoch_*.ckpt) assert any("best.ckpt" in str(p) for p in paths)
task.implementation:
1. Add helper function to src/minivess/orchestration/flows/post_training_flow.py:
def resolve_checkpoint_paths_from_contract( *, parent_run_id: str | None, tracking_uri: str, ) -> list[Path]: """Resolve checkpoint .ckpt files from FlowContract fold tags.
Calls FlowContract.find_fold_checkpoints(parent_run_id) to get checkpoint_dir paths, then globs for .ckpt files in each dir. Prefers best.ckpt over epoch_*.ckpt when both exist. Returns empty list on any failure. """ if parent_run_id is None: return [] from minivess.orchestration.flow_contract import FlowContract fc = FlowContract(tracking_uri=tracking_uri) fold_info = fc.find_fold_checkpoints(parent_run_id=parent_run_id) if not fold_info: return [] paths = [] for fold in fold_info: ckpt_dir = Path(fold["checkpoint_dir"]) if not ckpt_dir.exists(): logger.warning("checkpoint_dir %s does not exist", ckpt_dir) continue # Prefer best.ckpt best = ckpt_dir / "best.ckpt" if best.exists(): paths.append(best) continue # Fallback: last epoch_*.ckpt sorted by name epoch_ckpts = sorted(ckpt_dir.glob("epoch_*.ckpt")) if epoch_ckpts: paths.append(epoch_ckpts[-1]) return paths
2. In run_post_training_flow() in post_training_flow.py, update the section that builds checkpoint_paths to call resolve_checkpoint_paths_from_contract when checkpoint_paths is None:
After finding upstream_training_run_id, add: if not checkpoint_paths: checkpoint_paths = resolve_checkpoint_paths_from_contract( parent_run_id=upstream_training_run_id, tracking_uri=tracking_uri, ) if checkpoint_paths: log.info( "Resolved %d checkpoint(s) from FlowContract", len(checkpoint_paths), ) else: log.warning( "No checkpoints found via FlowContract for run %s — " "all plugins will skip", upstream_training_run_id, )
task.acceptance: uv run pytest tests/unit/orchestration/test_post_training_contract.py -v All 3 tests pass. uv run ruff check src/minivess/orchestration/flows/post_training_flow.py

task.name: analysis_flow uses resolve_experiment_name
task.description:
RED: Write tests verifying analysis_flow does NOT hardcode "minivess_training". GREEN: Replace the two hardcoded occurrences in analysis_flow.py.
Locations in analysis_flow.py (verified from code): - Line 1698: experiment_name="minivess_training" - Line 1707: mlflow.set_experiment("minivess_training")
Both must use resolve_experiment_name("minivess_training") instead. Also: UPSTREAM_EXPERIMENT env var should override the resolved name (already partially present — wire it through properly).
task.test-first:
Create tests/unit/orchestration/test_analysis_flow_experiment_name.py
def test_analysis_flow_uses_resolve_experiment_name(monkeypatch): """analysis_flow source must not contain hardcoded 'minivess_training'.""" import ast import inspect from minivess.orchestration.flows import analysis_flow as af_module
source = inspect.getsource(af_module) # Parse the source — no regex (Rule #16) tree = ast.parse(source) # Look for string constants "minivess_training" in the AST hardcoded = [] for node in ast.walk(tree): if isinstance(node, ast.Constant) and node.value == "minivess_training": hardcoded.append(node) assert len(hardcoded) == 0, ( f"Found {len(hardcoded)} hardcoded 'minivess_training' string(s) " f"in analysis_flow.py — use resolve_experiment_name() instead" )
def test_analysis_flow_reads_debug_suffix_from_env(monkeypatch): """When MINIVESS_DEBUG_SUFFIX=_DEBUG, analysis queries minivess_training_DEBUG.""" monkeypatch.setenv("MINIVESS_DEBUG_SUFFIX", "_DEBUG") from minivess.orchestration.constants import resolve_experiment_name name = resolve_experiment_name("minivess_training") assert name == "minivess_training_DEBUG"
def test_upstream_experiment_env_overrides_resolved_name(monkeypatch): """UPSTREAM_EXPERIMENT env var must take priority over resolve_experiment_name.""" monkeypatch.setenv("UPSTREAM_EXPERIMENT", "my_custom_experiment") # analysis_flow reads UPSTREAM_EXPERIMENT; if set, use it directly import os from minivess.orchestration.constants import resolve_experiment_name upstream_exp = os.environ.get( "UPSTREAM_EXPERIMENT", resolve_experiment_name("minivess_training") ) assert upstream_exp == "my_custom_experiment"
task.implementation:
In src/minivess/orchestration/flows/analysis_flow.py:
1. Add import at top (after existing imports): from minivess.orchestration.constants import resolve_experiment_name
2. Find the section near line 1698 where experiment_name is hardcoded. Read lines 1690-1720 of analysis_flow.py to identify the exact pattern. Replace hardcoded "minivess_training" with: upstream_exp = os.environ.get( "UPSTREAM_EXPERIMENT", resolve_experiment_name("minivess_training"), )
3. Replace the mlflow.set_experiment("minivess_training") at line 1707 with: mlflow.set_experiment(resolve_experiment_name("minivess_analysis")) (analysis flow writes to its own experiment, not to training)
NOTE: Read lines 1680-1730 of analysis_flow.py carefully before making changes — the logic may be more complex than these two simple replacements.
task.acceptance: uv run pytest tests/unit/orchestration/test_analysis_flow_experiment_name.py -v All 3 tests pass. uv run ruff check src/minivess/orchestration/flows/analysis_flow.py (mypy may have pre-existing errors in analysis_flow.py — only fix new ones)

task.name: Phase 0 full test run + commit
task.description: Run the full test suite for the affected files. Verify no regressions. Commit Phase 0 changes. Update state/tdd-state.json. Close the GitHub issue for Phase 0.
task.verify:
uv run pytest tests/unit/orchestration/ -v --tb=short uv run ruff check src/minivess/orchestration/ uv run mypy src/minivess/orchestration/flow_contract.py src/minivess/orchestration/constants.py
Expected: all new tests pass, no regressions in existing tests. If pre-existing failures exist: create GitHub issue, DO NOT block Phase 0 commit.
task.commit-message:
fix(connectivity): wire train→post-training→analysis MLflow contract
- constants.py: add resolve_experiment_name() (reads MINIVESS_DEBUG_SUFFIX) - flow_contract.py: _debug_suffix from env; find_fold_checkpoints(); log_flow_completion() writes FLOW_COMPLETE (was 'completed') + accepts checkpoint_dir kwarg - train_flow.py: log checkpoint_dir_fold_{N} tags per fold; add checkpoint_dirs to TrainingFlowResult - post_training_flow.py: add resolve_checkpoint_paths_from_contract(); call it when checkpoint_paths is None - analysis_flow.py: replace hardcoded "minivess_training" with resolve_experiment_name(); write to minivess_analysis experiment
Fixes: post-training plugins no longer skip (0 checkpoints → real paths). Analysis now queries correct experiment name with debug suffix support.
Closes #[PHASE0_ISSUE]

phase.name: Data Engineering → Train Handoff
phase.description:
Wire data_flow.py to write splits_path as an MLflow tag so that train_flow.py can discover and use the correct data splits from the previous data engineering run.
Currently: data_flow.py writes to mlflow experiment "minivess_data" but never writes the splits_path tag. train_flow.py calls find_upstream_safely() which always returns None (no FLOW_COMPLETE tag in the data experiment).

task.name: Tests for data_flow splits_path MLflow tag
task.description: Write failing tests verifying data_flow writes splits_path tag on FLOW_COMPLETE.
task.test-first:
Create tests/unit/orchestration/test_data_flow_contract.py
def test_data_flow_writes_splits_path_tag(tmp_path, monkeypatch): """run_data_flow must set splits_path tag on the MLflow run.""" from unittest.mock import patch, MagicMock from minivess.orchestration.flows.data_flow import run_data_flow from mlflow.tracking import MlflowClient import mlflow
monkeypatch.setenv("MINIVESS_ALLOW_HOST", "1") tracking_uri = str(tmp_path / "mlruns") mlflow.set_tracking_uri(tracking_uri) monkeypatch.setenv("SPLITS_DIR", str(tmp_path / "splits"))
data_dir = tmp_path / "data" data_dir.mkdir()
# Create minimal fake data pairs with patch( "minivess.orchestration.flows.data_flow.discover_data_task" ) as mock_discover: mock_discover.return_value = [ {"image": str(tmp_path / "img1.nii"), "label": str(tmp_path / "lbl1.nii")} ] * 5 with patch( "minivess.orchestration.flows.data_flow.dvc_pull_task", side_effect=Exception("dvc not installed"), ): result = run_data_flow(data_dir=data_dir, n_folds=2, seed=42)
assert result.splits_path is not None, "splits_path must be set in result"
# Check MLflow tag was written client = MlflowClient(tracking_uri=tracking_uri) experiments = client.search_experiments() data_exp = next( (e for e in experiments if "minivess_data" in e.name), None ) assert data_exp is not None, "minivess_data experiment must exist" runs = client.search_runs([data_exp.experiment_id]) assert len(runs) >= 1 run = runs[0] assert "splits_path" in run.data.tags, ( "data_flow must write splits_path tag for train_flow to discover" ) assert "flow_status" in run.data.tags assert run.data.tags["flow_status"] == "FLOW_COMPLETE"
def test_data_flow_writes_flow_complete_tag(tmp_path, monkeypatch): """run_data_flow must set flow_status=FLOW_COMPLETE (not 'completed').""" # Same setup as above, just check flow_status value # (tests that T0.2's fix to log_flow_completion propagates here too) pass # merge into test above or test separately
task.acceptance: uv run pytest tests/unit/orchestration/test_data_flow_contract.py -v Tests FAIL before T1.2 implementation (splits_path tag not written).

task.name: data_flow.py writes splits_path tag to MLflow
task.description: Update data_flow.py so that when splits are serialized, the path is written as an MLflow tag on the flow completion run. Use log_flow_completion() from FlowContract (already imported in data_flow.py via log_completion_safe).
task.implementation:
In src/minivess/orchestration/flows/data_flow.py:
Find the section where mlflow.start_run() is called (around line 530). After `mlflow_run_id = active_run.info.run_id`, add: if splits_path is not None: mlflow.set_tag("splits_path", str(splits_path))
Also find the log_completion_safe() call at the bottom and verify it writes "FLOW_COMPLETE" (after T0.2 fix). If not, update accordingly.
Also ensure the experiment name uses resolve_experiment_name(): Replace mlflow.set_experiment("minivess_data") with: mlflow.set_experiment(resolve_experiment_name("minivess_data")) Add import: from minivess.orchestration.constants import resolve_experiment_name
task.acceptance: uv run pytest tests/unit/orchestration/test_data_flow_contract.py -v All tests pass. uv run ruff check src/minivess/orchestration/flows/data_flow.py

task.name: Phase 1 commit + close issue
task.verify: uv run pytest tests/unit/orchestration/ -v --tb=short uv run ruff check src/minivess/orchestration/
task.commit-message:
feat(connectivity): data-flow writes splits_path tag to MLflow
- data_flow.py: write splits_path MLflow tag after split serialization - data_flow.py: use resolve_experiment_name("minivess_data") for debug suffix - Flow contract: data experiment now discoverable by train_flow
Closes #[PHASE1_ISSUE]
